title: Compound-specific tyre ageing hazard bins
summary: Represent pit hazard as a function of compound-specific tyre age using coarse bins, old-for-compound flags, and tyre-age interactions with race phase.
rationale: Raw target behavior is strongly tied to tyre age and stint state, and the run showed tyre-pressure features as consistently useful. Binned hazard features can capture non-linear age effects that a single ratio may miss.
implementation_hint: For each compound class, create coarse TyreLife bins, old/very-old flags based on fixed compound thresholds, TyreLife times late-race phase, and TyreLife divided by expected compound life. Test separately from final-stop feasibility to isolate whether age-hazard features add information.
expected_effect: Small to medium AUC gain with medium confidence.
risk: Overly fine bins can overfit race- and season-specific stint distributions. Use a small number of bins and let tree models learn interactions.
